_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-1 Pediatric Medication Emergency Dosing and Intervention Cards (Revised 01/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template.
condition: Pediatric Medication Emergency Dosing and Intervention Cards (MI-MEDIC)
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-1: Pediatric Medication Emergency Dosing and Intervention Cards (MI-MEDIC) — MDHHS Approved 1/27/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Instructions for using the Michigan Medication Emergency Dosing and Intervention Cards (MI-MEDIC). Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage. When unavailable, utilize pediatric dosing listed within the relevant protocol.

sections.title: MI-MEDIC Usage Instructions
points: Obtain correct weight of the child: (a) if patient's actual weight is known, use MI-MEDIC card for that weight — DO NOT CONFUSE POUNDS and KILOGRAMS; (b) if patient's weight is not known, use length-based resuscitation tape to determine the proper color zone; (c) if a length-based resuscitation tape is not available, use patient's age to determine color of card to use — DO NOT GUESS THE WEIGHT OF THE CHILD
Select appropriate weight-based medication for intervention
Select the corresponding colored card
Select desired medication from Cardiac Resuscitation or Medical Conditions
ASSURE medication CONCENTRATION on hand is as specified on card
Some medications should be diluted as instructed on card
If dilution is required, follow steps to dilute entire vial of medication prior to drawing up final mL volume to administer
Confirm medication dose and volume to be delivered
Administer volume of medication as desired
Contact Medical Control for questions or concerns

NOTE: Some medication doses have been rounded for safety and ease of use for the prevention of medication errors. These doses may not exactly correspond with the mg/kg dose in the pediatric treatment protocols. The use of these rounded doses has been approved for use and administration will be acceptable as long as the dose was referenced from the MI-MEDIC cards.

sections.title: MI-MEDIC Color Zones
points: Grey: 0–2 months, 3–5 kg (6–11 lbs.)
Pink: 3–6 months, 6–7 kg (13–16 lbs.)
Red: 7–10 months, 8–9 kg (17–20 lbs.)
Purple: 11–18 months, 10–11 kg (21–25 lbs.)
Yellow: 19–35 months, 12–14 kg (26–31 lbs.)
White: 3–4 years, 15–18 kg (32–40 lbs.)
Blue: 5–6 years, 19–23 kg (41–50 lbs.)
Orange: 7–9 years, 24–29 kg (52–64 lbs.)
Green: 10–14 years, 30–36 kg (65–79 lbs.)

scope_notes: MI-MEDIC reference system: all levels for pediatric patients < 14 years
Always use MI-MEDIC when available for pediatric dosing — protocol tables are backup only
When MI-MEDIC unavailable: use age/weight dosing tables in individual pediatric treatment protocols
